commit.message:
fix(build): prevent 0-byte static pages from I/O contention

- Reduce parallel write batch from 300 to 200 to lower I/O pressure
- Add sync repair pass after flush: detects and rewrites 0-byte files
- Log individual write failures with file paths (was only logging count)
- flatContentPlugin: skip copying 0-byte source files to flat paths

Root cause: writeFile uses O_TRUNC which creates 0-byte files before
writing content. Under heavy I/O contention (153K+ parallel writes),
some writes fail after truncation, leaving 0-byte files that Google
indexes as soft-404s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

(Rilevazione svizzera della struttura dei salari, cubi_RSS_02, 2024).

  match USTAT Ticino figures (generally lower than previous estimates)

Notable corrections (sectors most affected):
- Education: was overstated by 20-36% (Ticino education pays less)
- Engineering: junior/mid were 10-18% too high
- Hospitality: senior was overstated by ~29%
- Finance senior: was understated by ~44% (USTAT shows CHF 184k)
- Retail: was understated by 18-37% across all levels

commit.message:
fix: suppress uv hardlink warning with UV_LINK_MODE=copy (#65)

When running as a GitHub Action, the uv cache and target .venv directory
end up on different filesystems (container overlay vs host mount), causing
uv to fail hardlinking and emit a noisy warning on every run. Setting
UV_LINK_MODE=copy tells uv to use copy mode directly, suppressing the
warning.

Resolves the remaining part of #27.

Signed-off-by: Zack Koppert <zkoppert@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feature/add metrics (#12)

Add comprehensive metrics across all sink components:

KustoSinkCommon (non-WAL path):
- bytesSent counter: tracks CSV bytes written to blob storage
- blobsUploaded counter: tracks blob upload count
- ingestionLatencyMs histogram: tracks ingestion polling latency

KustoPrecommittingSinkWriter (two-phase commit writer):
- numBytesSend (Flink built-in): tracks CSV bytes during prepareCommit
- blobsUploaded counter: tracks blob uploads per commit
- blobUploadLatencyMs histogram: tracks blob upload + serialization time

KustoSinkCommitter (two-phase commit committer):
- ingestionSucceeded counter: tracks successful ingestions
- ingestionFailed counter: tracks failed ingestions
- ingestionLatencyMs histogram: tracks ingestion completion latency

Uses Flink DescriptiveStatisticsHistogram (window=1000) for histograms,
matching the ClickHouse connector pattern. Metrics are exposed via JMX
under the standard Flink metric group hierarchy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Docker quickstart for Kafka-to-Kusto Flink pipeline

Docker Compose setup with Kafka (Strimzi KRaft) + Flink (JobManager +
TaskManager) that reads JSON sensor readings from Kafka and writes them
to Azure Data Explorer via flink-connector-kusto.

Components:
- docker-compose.yml: Kafka broker + Flink cluster (3 services)
- flink-job/: Standalone Maven project with KafkaSource -> KustoWriteSink
- build.sh: Builds connector JAR, job JAR, and Docker images
- submit-job.sh: Uploads and submits the Flink job via REST API
- produce-messages.sh: Generates sample JSON messages to Kafka
- kusto-config/create-table.kql: Kusto table creation script
- README.md: Step-by-step quickstart guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add one-shot run.sh wrapper with burst message loading

Single script that builds, starts services, submits the Flink job, and
produces messages in configurable bursts (default: 500 msgs in 5 bursts
of 100, with 15s pauses). Includes pre-flight validation of .env vars.

Usage: ./run.sh [total_messages] [num_bursts]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Grafana and Kusto Web Explorer dashboards with OTEL metrics pipeline

Monitoring stack for the Flink Kusto connector quickstart:

OTEL Collector:
- Scrapes Flink Prometheus metrics from JobManager and TaskManager
- Filters to Kusto sink metrics, Flink operator metrics, and checkpoint metrics
- Exports to Azure Data Explorer OTELMetrics table

Grafana Dashboard (flink-kusto-metrics.json):
- Records sent, bytes sent, ingestion success/failure time series
- Blobs uploaded counter
- Ingestion latency histogram (p50/p95/p99)
- Blob upload latency histogram
- Current send time gauge
- Flink job health: uptime, checkpoint stats
- All metrics table view
- Auto-provisioned datasource and dashboard on startup

Kusto Web Explorer Dashboard (kusto-dashboard.json):
- Two pages: Kusto Sink metrics and Flink Job Health
- Same metric panels as Grafana, using native KQL queries
- Importable directly into Kusto Web Explorer

Docker Compose additions:
- Flink Prometheus metric reporter on both JobManager and TaskManager
- OTEL Collector Contrib container
- Grafana with ADX plugin auto-provisioned
- OTELMetrics table in create-table.kql

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update run.sh to include full monitoring pipeline in summary

Add step 6 to verify Flink Prometheus metrics endpoint is active before
showing summary. Include Grafana URL, Prometheus URL, and OTELMetrics
query examples in the final output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix checkpoint storage failure in Docker Flink setup

Use /tmp/flink-checkpoints instead of a Docker volume mount at
/opt/flink/checkpoints. The FileSystemCheckpointStorage was failing
because the directory didn't exist when the job initialized.

Also set state.backend: hashmap explicitly and remove the unused
flink-checkpoints volume.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix NoClassDefFoundError: use shaded connector JAR in quickstart

The dependency-reduced-pom.xml from the shade plugin strips transitive
dependencies, so the quickstart job was missing Kusto SDK classes at
runtime. Use classifier=shaded to pull the fat JAR that bundles all
dependencies (kusto-ingest, azure-storage, etc).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve troubleshooting for Kusto connectivity from Docker

Add guidance for 'Unable to get ingestion resources' timeout error:
cluster URL must be engine URL (not ingest), Docker needs outbound
internet access, Service Principal needs Ingestor role. Added DNS
config hint for corporate proxy/VPN environments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Shade and relocate dependencies to avoid Flink classpath conflicts

Relocate Jackson, Azure SDK, Netty, Reactor, Nimbus, and other transitive
dependencies under the flink_kusto_shaded prefix. This prevents
NoSuchMethodError on BufferRecycler.releaseToPool() caused by Flink's
bundled older Jackson conflicting with the Kusto SDK's requirements.

Add jackson-bom to dependencyManagement to ensure all Jackson modules
resolve to 2.21.0 (flink-connector-kafka pulled in jackson-core 2.15.2).

Add explicit commons-text dependency (was provided-scope transitive from
Kusto SDK, causing NoClassDefFoundError at runtime).

Modeled after the azure-kusto-spark connector's shading approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cache ContainerProvider as instance field to reduce DM calls

ContainerProvider was being created new per batch in both KustoSinkCommon
and KustoPrecommittingSinkWriter, discarding the internal 2-hour TTL cache
and hitting the DM for ingestion resources on every single batch. This also
created a new QueuedIngestClient (with its own ResourceManager) each time.

Fix: promote ContainerProvider to an instance field initialized once in the
constructor, matching the pattern already used for IngestClient.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix latency dashboard queries to match Flink histogram metric names

Flink's Prometheus reporter emits histogram quantiles as separate metrics
with suffixes like '.0.5', '.0.99', '.0.999', 'count' — not 'Mean',
'Median', 'p95', 'p99' as the dashboard queries assumed. Updated both
Grafana and Kusto Web Explorer dashboards to:
- Use a broader regex pattern [._] to match actual metric suffixes
- Map quantile values (0.5, 0.95, 0.99) to friendly labels (p50, p95, p99)
- Handle both Prometheus quantile format and named stat format

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Increase batch size to 20k records for quickstart

Set batchSize to 20000 in KafkaToKustoJob (was using default 1000).
Updated run.sh default to 20000 messages in 5 bursts and
produce-messages.sh default to 20000 messages with 2000 per batch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove non-existent blobUploadLatencyMs from dashboards

blobUploadLatencyMs only exists in KustoPrecommittingSinkWriter (two-phase
commit path). The quickstart uses AT_LEAST_ONCE which goes through
KustoSinkCommon/KustoSinkWriter. Replaced the blob upload latency panel
with currentSendTime and removed the duplicate send latency panel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ingestion counters always 0 when polling is disabled

succeededIngestions, failedIngestions, partialSucceededIngestions, and
ingestionLatencyMs were only incremented inside the polling callback.
When pollForIngestionStatus=false (the default), the non-polling path
returned true without updating any counters.

Now the non-polling path increments succeededIngestions and records
the queue latency (upload+queue time). The error catch path also
increments failedIngestions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename ingestion counters: successfullyIngested vs successfullyQueued

- succeededIngestions renamed to successfullyIngested (only incremented
  when polling confirms ingestion succeeded)
- New successfullyQueued counter for the non-polling path (queued but
  ingestion status not verified)
- failedIngestions and partialSucceededIngestions unchanged
- Updated Grafana and Kusto dashboards to use has_any() for matching
  the new metric names

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix arg_max query: Timestamp not resolvable in project

arg_max(Timestamp, MetricValue) doesn't preserve Timestamp as a named
column for project. Replaced with max(Timestamp) + max(MetricValue)
in both Grafana and Kusto dashboards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[release] Fix multi-line stdout breaking GITHUB_OUTPUT

uv version --bump patch prints an info line to stdout that gets
captured alongside the version string, producing a multi-line value
that breaks the GITHUB_OUTPUT format in the release workflow.

Suppress uv version stdout with capture_output=True so only the
clean semver string is printed.
